TAMAZIA · ABOUT

Aman Pareek

Founder, Tamazia

LLM in International Business Law, King’s College London
Member, Chartered Institute of Arbitrators

The Foundation

Tamazia was built on a specific observation: regulated enterprises face a compliance gap that generic SEO agencies cannot close. Every piece of content, every service page, every case study, every blog post, touches a regulatory framework. Most agencies treat compliance as a constraint. Tamazia treats it as the methodology.

The firm is led by a founder with an LLM in International Business Law from King’s College London, with a specialism in business law and international arbitration. Membership of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators provides access to institutional frameworks and practitioner networks that inform how Tamazia approaches regulated content.

Earlier exposure to the Supreme Court of India's constitutional chambers, and work across ICC and LCIA arbitration proceedings, established the discipline that underpins Tamazia's compliance review process: the same rigour applied to a legal argument applies to a published webpage. What is written in public becomes the record.

Tamazia's clients include an NSE-listed hotel group, CG Oncology (Nasdaq: CGON), and Meraas, a Dubai Holding subsidiary. Those engagements were not won on the basis of technical SEO credentials alone. They were won on the basis that the firm could produce content that survives regulatory scrutiny in the jurisdictions where those clients operate: the United Kingdom, the United Arab Emirates, the United States, and the European Union simultaneously.

Credentials

  • Education LLM in International Business Law, King’s College London
  • Specialism Business law and international arbitration
  • Memberships Chartered Institute of Arbitrators (CIArb)
  • Institutional exposure ICC · LCIA · UNCITRAL · Supreme Court of India
  • Jurisdictions United Kingdom · United Arab Emirates · United States · European Union

Beyond Tamazia

Aman Pareek is also the founder of LexQuity, a legal technology company building the first AI-native research and document infrastructure for the international arbitration market.

Work with Tamazia

Every engagement opens with a regulatory and SEO audit against the standards applicable to your sector. No commitment required to begin.

Begin an audit enquiry →

Responses within one business day. All communications confidential.
